repo_prior_art.cite: Closest API entries are Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis and Q1_SpectralThreshold; neither states an active pre-period EIF rank boundary for BJS/SA/CS/W.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenSantAnnaXie2025: closed-form EIFs, overidentification, and optimal weighting of multiple pre-periods; rank-zero equality frontier is not stated but is very close projection algebra.
none; generic Weyl/Davis-Kahan rank consistency once H_pre is defined.

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest rejected same-qid angle used a heterogeneity-matrix K_star frontier, not this observable pre-period score residual.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenSantAnnaXie2025 and BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024: efficient EIF/imputation comparisons already cover most variance-bound substrate; the named four-estimator classifier remains unstated.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; ChenSantAnnaXie2025 already gives the full EIF, overidentification, and optimal pre-period weighting, so the current rank-zero condition reads as a direct projection-algebra corollary unless tied to actual named estimator formulas.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal treats BJS as attaining the arbitrary-Sigma full semiparametric pre-period bound, but ChenSantAnnaXie2025 explicitly says the BJS-G-W estimator has no efficiency guarantees outside its specific additional error-structure setup.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The load-bearing objects b_a, B_SA, B_CS, and B_W are only described verbally; the proposal says finite matrices are defined in Section 6, but no indexed entries or dimensions are actually pinned.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The named-estimator implication assumes common SA/CS/W baseline spans and full-projection BJS behavior, so the theorem is about stipulated projection spans rather than the published estimator implementations unless the matrices are made explicit.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define B_SA, B_CS, B_W, B_all, and b_a with explicit indices and dimensions for a finite staggered panel, including the exact comparison-support convention.
Add a comparison proposition mapping ChenSantAnnaXie2025's EIF weights to H_pre and identifying exactly which equality condition is absent from their paper.
For flagship eligibility, remove the common-baseline assumption and prove separate necessary-and-sufficient H_pre,e frontiers for the actual BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge influence functions.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal to pin the estimator matrices and reframe the novelty as a concrete non-subsumed equality frontier relative to ChenSantAnnaXie2025, not as abstract projection residual rank.
